title: dendrorkis odorata
intro_study: According to the study, dendrorkis odorata, likely a misspelling of Dendrobium, benefits include having been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its tonic, astringent, analgesic, antipyretic, and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Dendrorkis odorata can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's extracts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, and may also interact with certain medications. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils has been linked to headaches and dizziness, while excessive use can cause liver and kidney damage. Improper use of the plant can also exacerbate pre-existing conditions, such as asthma or eczema.
